en: Come down and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon; sit on the ground without a throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans! For you shall no more be called tender and delicate.
zh: 巴比倫的處女啊！ 你下來，坐在塵土中吧！ 迦勒底的女兒啊！ 你坐在地上吧！沒有寶座了， 因為你必不再被稱為柔弱嬌嫩的了。

en: Take the millstones and grind flour, put off your veil, strip off your robe, uncover your legs, pass through the rivers.
zh: 你要拿石磨去磨麵， 你要揭開你的帕子，脫去長裙， 露出大腿，渡過江河。

en: Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your disgrace shall be seen. I will take vengeance, and I will spare no one.
zh: 你的下體必暴露出來， 你的羞恥要被人看見； 我必報仇，不顧惜任何人。

en: Our Redeemer- the LORD of hosts is his name- is the Holy One of Israel.
zh: 我們的救贖主，他的名字是萬軍之耶和華、 以色列的聖者，他說：

en: Sit in silence, and go into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans; for you shall no more be called the mistress of kingdoms.
zh: “迦勒底的女子啊！ 你要靜靜坐下，進到黑暗中去， 因為人不再稱你為列國之后了。

en: I was angry with my people; I profaned my heritage; I gave them into your hand; you showed them no mercy; on the aged you made your yoke exceedingly heavy.
zh: 我曾對我的子民發怒， 使我的產業被褻瀆， 我把他們交在你的手裡， 你卻對他們毫無憐憫。 你竟把你的軛，重重壓在老年人的身上。

en: You said, "I shall be mistress forever," so that you did not lay these things to heart or remember their end.
zh: 你曾說：‘我必永遠作王后！’ 所以你不把這些事放在心上， 也不思想這事的結局。

en: Now therefore hear this, you lover of pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, "I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children":
zh: 因此，你這淫逸、安居的， 現在要聽這話。 你曾心裡說： ‘只有我，除我以外再沒有別的； 我必不會寡居， 也不會經歷喪子的事。’

en: These two things shall come to you in a moment, in one day; the loss of children and widowhood shall come upon you in full measure, in spite of your many sorceries and the great power of your enchantments.
zh: 不料喪子和寡居這兩件事， 竟在忽然之間，一日之內，要臨到你身上！ 正在你多行邪術， 竭力施符咒的時候， 這兩件事就必都臨到你身上。

en: You felt secure in your wickedness, you said, "No one sees me"; your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, and you said in your heart, "I am, and there is no one besides me."
zh: 你向來倚靠自己的邪惡， 說：‘沒有人看見我’； 你的智慧和知識把你引入了歧途。 你心裡說： ‘只有我，除了我以外，再沒有別的。’

en: But evil shall come upon you, which you will not know how to charm away; disaster shall fall upon you, for which you will not be able to atone; and ruin shall come upon you suddenly, of which you know nothing.
zh: 但災禍必臨到你身上， 你卻不知道它的來源； 禍患必落在你身上， 你卻不能把它除去； 毀滅必忽然臨到你身上， 你卻不知道。

en: Stand fast in your enchantments and your many sorceries, with which you have labored from your youth; perhaps you may be able to succeed; perhaps you may inspire terror.
zh: 繼續使用你從幼年時就勞碌 施行的符咒 和許多的邪術吧！ 或者你可以得到益處， 也許你能叫人戰慄。

en: You are wearied with your many counsels; let them stand forth and save you, those who divide the heavens, who gaze at the stars, who at the new moons make known what shall come upon you.
zh: 你因你許多的計劃而勞累； 現在讓那些劃分天象的， 觀看星辰的， 在月朔時說預言的， 都站起來，拯救你脫離要臨到你身上的事。

en: Behold, they are like stubble; the fire consumes them; they cannot deliver themselves from the power of the flame. No coal for warming oneself is this, no fire to sit before!
zh: 看哪！他們必像碎稭， 火必要焚燒他們； 他們不能救自己脫離火燄的威力； 這不是可以烤火取暖的火炭， 也不是可以坐在它面前的火。

en: Such to you are those with whom you have labored, who have done business with you from your youth; they wander about each in his own direction; there is no one to save you.
zh: 你所勞碌的事，都要這樣與你無益； 從你年幼時與你交易的， 也都必各奔各方，四處飄流， 沒有人拯救你。”
